name: yao-deepseek-crawler
title: Yao DeepSeek Crawler
job_to_be_done: Run multiple DeepSeek web AI-search queries repeatedly, normalize the crawl records, and turn them into GEO probability evidence.

trigger_surface.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
should_trigger: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
should_not_trigger: Do not handle generic website crawling, DeepSeek API-only calls, unrelated SEO reports, or one-off question answering.

decision_points: Ask one focused clarification when the real job, output, or exclusion boundary is unclear.
Escalate visible tradeoffs when benchmark patterns conflict with local privacy, naming, or governance constraints.
Do not silently broaden the skill into adjacent jobs just because the examples are nearby.
failure_modes: Headings and summaries can drift into generic, interchangeable language.
The output can sound polished but lose the user's actual taste, audience, or scenario.
Tables can render as dense grids with weak hierarchy or poor mobile readability.
Long bullets can make the output look complete while hiding the actual decision logic.
Footnote markers or dense citation notes can interrupt the reading flow.
Evidence can be over-attached to obvious statements and under-attached to risky claims.
Users start invoking the skill for adjacent one-off or explanation-only requests.
Outputs remain valid but become generic, cluttered, or weakly aligned with the user's domain.
Borrowed benchmark patterns no longer fit the local job or add ceremony without payoff.
Skill usage becomes team-critical while ownership, review cadence, or rollback evidence stays informal.
